floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: BOTH flagged constructs assessed inert -- the always-true-if folds to the plain early-return by cse+jump (target bytes s:3-6 are exactly the plain form's shape), and duplicate file-

frontier.hypothesis: The single-edit redo -- `if (v0 <= 0) return 0;` replacing the wrapped form, duplicate extern sets (main.c:1685-1691) and `int new_var2;` deleted -- keeps sandbox 0 and the oracle SHA1 unchanged.
frontier.mechanism: cse.c const-prop + jump.c dead-branch deletion already reduce the committed form to exactly this RTL; duplicate_decls merge makes the redecl deletion a no-op by construction.
frontier.next_probe: Apply the edit, run `& tools/wteng.ps1 main sandbox spu_IrqHandler --disable all` (expect 0), then verify-oracle --rebuild and recommit as cheat-cleanup.

frontier.hypothesis: CONTINGENCY (only if probe 1 diverges): the divergence would be in branch sense or return-path layout (jump.c invert-jump / reorg.c delay-slot fill around the bgtz), not in the body -- and the lever space is the exit-form spelling catalog (goto-end vs inline return, switch-break-shared-return-sched-hoist / cross-jump-store-tail-merge families), never the always-true-if.
frontier.mechanism: reorg.c relax_delay_slots + jump.c branch inversion choose between bgtz/blez and inline-vs-shared return-0 placement based on block layout.
frontier.next_probe: Diff the sandbox asm's first 6 insns against func_80089384.s:2-6; if flipped, sweep exit spellings (inline `return 0;` / `goto end` variants) in one sweep_variants call before touching anything else.

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Two cheats in src/main.c:1693-1710. Primary: 'new_var2 = 1; if (new_var2) { return 0; }' (lines 1698-1701) is an always-true-if / dead-conditional-store — explicitly forbidden family. Secondary: duplicate extern declarations of g_spu_voice_key_{a,b,c} at lines 1685-1691 (two identical back-to-back sets after the file-top declaration at lines 52-54) — reference-count inflation coercion. Function needs a clean pure-C redo that achieves the same byte-match without these constructs; a worker should rewrite the early-exit path as a plain 'if (a0 <= 0) return 0;' and drop the duplicate extern declarations, then drive back to sandbox-0 with pure-C levers.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
